title: Aureline Support Center information-architecture capability card and route contract
description: Boundary schema for the Support Center information architecture frozen in docs/support/support_center_information_architecture.md. Defines support_center_capability_card_record (one card per top-level Support Center module), support_center_route_record (one rule binding a closed symptom-surface class to a primary capability card and a default first action), and support_center_information_architecture_record (the index that pins the closed nine-module set, the symptom-surface vocabulary, the deployment-context overlay, and the no-upload-first invariant). Reuses the closed vocabularies already frozen in schemas/support/scenario_picker.schema.json, schemas/support/doctor_finding.schema.json, schemas/support/repair_transaction.schema.json, schemas/support/object_handoff_packet.schema.json, schemas/support/support_bundle.schema.json, and schemas/support/recovery_action.schema.json rather than minting parallel tokens. The intake-bound support_capability_card_record in scenario_picker.schema.json carries the picker-side parity rows; this schema carries the IA-side card whose richer field set covers purpose, read-only/mutating posture, confidence class, evidence sources, local-only/export actions, headless availability, policy constraints, and open-related-packet actions.

support_center_schema_version.description: Collection schema version for this Support Center IA contract.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short reviewer-facing sentence safe to render in Support Center previews, capability cards, and CLI/headless output. MUST NOT carry milestone or task identifiers.

stable_ref_id.description: Stable lowercase id that permits `:` as a prefix separator so card and route ids match the `support_center_card:<module>` and `support_center_route:<symptom>.<module>` fixture conventions.

support_center_module_class.description: Closed set of Support Center top-level modules required by the Support Center information architecture. Adding a token is breaking and requires a new decision row in artifacts/governance/decision_index.yaml.

support_capability_class.description: Re-exported from schemas/support/scenario_picker.schema.json. Names the intake-bound capability the IA module aligns with so the symptom-to-card route remains 1:1 with the picker capability registry. Modules that have no intake-bound capability (advisory_or_incident_history, field_diagnostics) declare aligned_intake_capability_class: null.

card_posture_class.description: Closed posture vocabulary. read_only_diagnosis covers Project Doctor and field diagnostics. read_only_evidence_review covers advisory or incident history and the support-bundle preview. mutating_with_review_and_preview covers guided repair and any rung that touches workspace, profile, or trust state. mutating_local_disposable_only covers safe mode, bisect, quarantine, and cache-reset candidates that only narrow runtime state. export_only_local_first covers the support-bundle export path. handoff_only_no_repair covers issue or escalation handoff. Adding a token is additive-minor.

confidence_class.description: Re-exported from schemas/support/doctor_finding.schema.json. Names the confidence ceiling the card presents on its primary target record.

evidence_source_class.description: Closed set of evidence sources a Support Center card may surface. Tokens reuse the existing artifact registry; advisory_record and policy_decision_log are reserved for advisory or incident history and field diagnostics. Adding a token is additive-minor.

local_action_class.description: Closed set of local-only actions a card may offer. Every card MUST declare at least one local-only action so a Support Center module is never reachable only through a network or upload step.

export_action_class.description: Closed set of export actions a card may offer. no_export_action MUST be the only entry on read-only cards that do not produce an export. Cards that produce an export MUST surface the bundle preview before the export leaves the device.

headless_availability_class.description: Closed availability vocabulary for the CLI/headless rendering of a card. Cards that declare unavailable_with_named_alternate MUST name an alternate path on the cli_headless context-parity row so a support flow never implies a desktop-only happy path.

policy_constraint_class.description: Closed set of policy constraints a card may carry. Cards that have no policy constraint declare no_constraint explicitly so an absent value never silently implies an unconstrained surface. Adding a token is additive-minor.

deployment_context_class.description: Closed set of deployment contexts a Support Center card and route MUST declare a parity row for. local_only is the user-installed individual baseline; managed names a fleet under a managed admin or control plane; self_hosted names a user-administered control plane or runtime; mirrored names an internal mirror or proxy of registries, docs, or update artifacts; offline names a fully air-gapped or temporarily disconnected device. Adding a token is breaking and requires a new decision row in artifacts/governance/decision_index.yaml.

deployment_availability_class.description: Re-exported from schemas/support/doctor_finding.schema.json availability_class so deployment-context parity uses the same availability tokens as Project Doctor.

open_related_packet_action_class.description: Closed set of open-related-packet actions a card may surface. Every card MUST declare at least one entry so a Support Center surface always names the next packet a reviewer can open instead of free-text follow-up copy.

symptom_surface_class.description: Closed set of in-product symptom surfaces from which Support Center routes start. Every Support Center route record MUST declare exactly one symptom_surface_class. Adding a token is breaking and requires a new decision row in artifacts/governance/decision_index.yaml.

default_first_action_class.description: Closed set of first actions a Support Center route may resolve to. refuse_route_no_upload_first is reserved for routes whose only network-bound option would otherwise be an upload-first path; the route MUST instead defer to a local-only diagnosis or evidence card.

narrowed_when_summary.description: One sentence that names the conditions under which the card or route is narrowed for this deployment context. Use 'not_narrowed' wording when the path is fully available.

alternate_path_summary.description: One sentence that names the alternate path (for example, local-only review, mirror-cached docs pack, deferred handoff) when the context is narrowed.

deployment_context_parity_row_array.description: Every Support Center capability card and every route record MUST carry exactly one parity row per deployment_context_class so a desktop-only or managed-only happy path cannot be implied.

primary_target_schema_ref.description: Optional repo-relative ref to the schema that boundary-checks this evidence source on this card.

target_schema_ref.description: Optional repo-relative ref to the schema that boundary-checks the opened packet.

purpose_summary.description: One sentence that states the card's purpose so the user does not have to read the contract docs to know what the module does.

confidence_class.description: Confidence ceiling the card presents on its primary target record. Cards with a read-only posture MUST NOT claim higher than the underlying probe or evidence supports.

primary_target_record_kind.description: Record kind the card hands the user to (for example doctor_finding_record, support_bundle_preview_item, recovery_action_record, repair_preview_record, runbook_packet, object_handoff_packet_record, support_escalation_packet_record, advisory_record, field_diagnostics_report).

primary_target_schema_ref.description: Repo-relative ref to the schema that boundary-checks the primary target record.

local_actions.description: Closed list of local-only actions the card offers. MUST include at least one entry so the module is reachable without a network or upload step.

export_actions.description: Closed list of export actions the card offers. Cards that produce no export MUST list no_export_action as the sole entry.

headless_availability_summary.description: One sentence that names how the card renders in CLI/headless and what alternate path is available when it is narrowed.

aligned_intake_capability_class.description: Names the intake-bound support_capability_class the card aligns with so the symptom-to-card route remains 1:1 with the picker capability registry. MUST be null on advisory_or_incident_history and field_diagnostics modules, which have no intake-bound capability.

linked_route_refs.description: Optional list of support_center_route_record ids that resolve to this card.

symptom_summary.description: One sentence that names the symptom surface the route starts from.

primary_module_class.description: The Support Center module the route resolves to first. Routes MUST resolve to the narrowest safe diagnostic or repair surface (Project Doctor, safe mode, bisect/quarantine, crash triage, or field diagnostics) before any export-only or handoff-only module.

secondary_module_classes.description: Closed list of follow-on modules the route may suggest after the primary module. MUST NOT lead with a module whose card_posture_class is mutating_with_review_and_preview unless the symptom surface is crash_loop_surface or update_failure_surface.

local_first_path_named.description: MUST be true on every route. The route MUST name a local-first path even when the symptom surface looks network-bound.

upload_required_for_first_action.description: MUST be false on every route. A route whose first action requires an upload is non-conforming and the route MUST instead resolve to refuse_route_no_upload_first or to a local-only diagnosis card.

scenario_family_ref_optional.description: Optional scenario_family_class the route preserves on the way to the picker so a later escalation packet does not re-mint a parallel family. Re-exported from schemas/support/scenario_picker.schema.json.

preserved_evidence_id_classes.description: Stable evidence-id classes the route preserves on the way to a card (for example doctor_finding_id, support_bundle_id, recovery_action_id, object_handoff_packet_id, advisory_record_id) so a later escalation packet can reconstruct the case.

linked_capability_card_ref.description: Optional stable id of the capability card the route resolves to. When present, the card's support_center_module_class MUST equal the route's primary_module_class.

module_classes_required.description: Every conforming Support Center IA MUST list all nine top-level module classes.

symptom_surface_classes_required.description: Every conforming Support Center IA MUST list all six symptom-surface classes.

deployment_context_classes_required.description: Every conforming Support Center IA MUST list all five deployment-context classes.

capability_card_refs.description: Stable capability_card_id references for the nine required Support Center modules.

route_refs.description: Stable route_id references for at least one route per symptom-surface class.

no_upload_first_invariant_summary.description: One sentence restating that no Support Center route resolves to an upload-first first action.
